Pictures shared via social media show flash flooding in the Clarkton community this evening. Water several inches deep over the road and in a parking lot near US-701 and N Mitchell Ford Rd. Water surrounding a home, yard, and outdoor storage containers at US-701 and W South Railrod St.
Portions of the Inlet Square Mall parking lot flooded. Water over the curb in spots.
Flash flooding along Wachesaw Road in Murrells Inlet. Mesonet reports show that over 4 inches of rain had fallen in that area over the previous 5-6 hours.
Minor flash flooding in the parking lots along Midway Park in Myrtle Beach.
Flash flooding along Parsonage Lane near the US-17 Business intersection.
Flash flooding along Belair Avenue in Murrells Inlet.
Flash flooding caused a section of South Waccamaw Drive to close about half a mile south of South Pointe Villas.

A tornado associated with Tropical Storm Debby produced concentrated damage starting at a property along Ash Little River Rd NW with several trees uprooted or snapped and minor damage to a home and fence. The tornado likely continued northwestward into a dense forest based on a tornado debris signature observed on radar.
Flash flooding reported along several residential roads in Cherry Grove Beach.
Flash flooding in a backyard just off the Sea Mountain Highway.
Spring Street is washed out along 20th Avenue North and 21st Avenue North in Cherry Grove Beach.
SC Highway Patrol reports road washout on Star Bluff Rd (north of Highway 90)

A weak tornado associated with Tropical Storm Debby caused concentrated tree damage in the town of Maple Hill in the vicinity of Old Maple Hill Rd N and NC-50. The tornado lifted after crossing NC-53.

Flood waters have been barricaded off along Factory Road in Coward.

Reports of flowing water on Sidbury Rd, Murrayville Rd, and several others in the northeast part of the county.

Video was received from the public at the north end of East Beach Drive of a small waterspout moving onshore. The video then showed the spout moving westward across the marshes until it dissipated after crossing an area of trees near Cape Creek Rd. There was no damage reported and it is estimated that this waterspout/tornado had winds of 60 mph.
Raleigh city official relayed a report of flash flooding ongoing along Federal Road South, Bald Head Wynd, and Sandspur Trail. Multiple golf course lagoons are flooding.
Flooding at Starfish Lane at Canal Drive
Flooded neighborhood streets along Myrtle Grove Road
Multiple flooded vehicles along Canal Drive
Widespread road flooding across St. James. Multiple ponds overflowing.
Floodwaters are two feet deep on US Highway 17 at Mile Marker 33. At least one vehicle is flooded.
Floodwater up to 2.5 feet deep on roads in the Rivermist neighborhood in Southport. Described as inchbad as Hurricane Florence. inch
Photos shared by the Southport Fire Dept show water approximately two feet deep in the Cades Cove subdivision of Southport

NWS survey team verified an EF-0 tornado touched down near the intersection of Columbus Rd and Stafford Rd, with max winds of 65 mph. The tornado tracked NNE, for 2.42 miles snapping a few trees and numerous limbs. More details will be available in a forthcoming Public Information Statement.
Damage consistent with a 75 mph/EF0 tornado observed to pine trees along US Hwy 701 in Hickory Grove Crossroads

Damage to trees and roofs consistent with an 80 mph/EF0 tornado was observed NW of Hickory Grove Crossroads along NC Hwy 210 and Peter Cromartie Rd. The tornado crossed the South River into Sampson County where EF1 damage was observed.
Helltown Road blocked by trees and power lines.
Trees down behind condo at Moss Vine Pl.
